tobacco.notes: Ongoing. Relevant to the involuntary-weight-loss differential (malignancy risk).

alcohol.amount: Heavy daily use (wine/liquor) for ~20 years → alcoholic cirrhosis. Not in remission at presentation.
alcohol.notes: Continued drinking up to this admission. Relevant to her cirrhosis decompensation and (separately) to transplant ineligibility at this time.

occupation.previous: Restaurant server / line cook for ~25 years.

housing.status: Rents a small apartment in Pittsburgh.
housing.notes: Somewhat socially isolated leading into this admission.

diet.type: Poor intake over the past several months; ~20 lb unintentional weight loss over 3 months.

functionalStatus.adls: Independent at baseline; declined with recent illness.
functionalStatus.iadls: Variable; some difficulty managing appointments and medications.

family.structure: Two adult children (a son and a daughter) from whom she had been ESTRANGED for several years. They re-engaged during this hospitalization. Her SISTER, Carol, has remained close and became the de facto medical decision-maker once the patient could not participate.
family.notes: The family dynamics are central to the later goals-of-care discussion: the sister is the surrogate; the reconciling adult children are emotionally involved. Clear, honest communication with this family is part of the case.

mentalHealth.depressionScreening: History of depression; not on treatment. Some hopelessness in the setting of isolation and illness.

notes: 55-year-old woman with active alcohol use disorder and decompensated cirrhosis, presenting with 3 months of involuntary weight loss. Socially isolated with recently reconciling family; her sister becomes the surrogate decision-maker. The social/family context matters most at the goals-of-care timepoint — and the case's core lesson (avoiding premature, irreversible decisions while a reversible, unconsidered diagnosis remains) plays out against that backdrop.
